# AID - AI Development Methodology
# Comprehensive LLM Context Documentation
# Version: 1.0.0
# Author: Ilan Dahan
# Last Updated: 2025-12-11

================================================================================
TABLE OF CONTENTS
================================================================================

1. Overview & Philosophy
2. The 5-Phase Gated Methodology (Detailed)
3. State Management System
4. Complete Command Reference
5. Skills System (Detailed)
6. MCP Integrations (Detailed)
7. Project Structure & Files
8. Technology Stack
9. Installation & Setup
10. Daily Workflow
11. Development Standards
12. Testing Standards
13. Atomic Design System
14. Code Quality Rules
15. Environment Configuration
16. Troubleshooting

================================================================================
1. OVERVIEW & PHILOSOPHY
================================================================================

AID (AI Development Methodology) is a comprehensive, end-to-end framework for
AI-assisted software development created by Ilan Dahan.

MISSION: Transform raw requirements into production-ready software using Claude
Code with integrated MCP (Model Context Protocol) services.

CORE PHILOSOPHY:
- Systematic, repeatable processes
- Built-in quality gates
- Continuous context tracking
- Phase-based workflow enforcement
- TDD-first development
- Atomic design principles

KEY DIFFERENTIATORS:
1. Phase gates prevent premature coding
2. Context tracking ensures session continuity
3. MCP integrations connect to real tools (Jira, Figma, GitHub)
4. Specialized skills for each development phase
5. Human approval required between phases

================================================================================
2. THE 5-PHASE GATED METHODOLOGY (DETAILED)
================================================================================

CRITICAL: Phases are strictly enforced. Claude MUST check `.aid/state.json`
before accepting ANY work request.

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE 1: PRD (Product Requirements Document)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Transform raw requirements into structured PRD

ALLOWED:
- Requirements gathering
- Scope definition
- User stories
- Acceptance criteria
- Stakeholder input analysis

BLOCKED:
- Code writing
- Architecture design
- Database schemas
- API design
- Jira issue creation

Input: Raw requirements, notes, stakeholder input
Output: docs/PRD.md, .aid/approvals/prd-approved.md

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE 2: Technical Specification
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Design system architecture and technical approach

ALLOWED:
- All Phase 1 work
- System architecture
- Database schema design
- API endpoint design
- Technology decisions
- Component architecture
- Security design

BLOCKED:
- Production code
- Jira issue creation
- Deployment

Input: Approved PRD
Output: docs/TECH-SPEC.md, .aid/approvals/tech-spec-approved.md

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE 3: Breakdown
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Break technical spec into actionable tasks

ALLOWED:
- All Phase 1-2 work
- Jira epic creation
- Story creation
- Task breakdown
- Estimation
- Sprint planning

BLOCKED:
- Production code
- Deployment

Input: Approved tech spec
Output: docs/JIRA-BREAKDOWN.md, .aid/approvals/breakdown-approved.md

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE 4: Development
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Implement features using TDD and atomic design

ALLOWED:
- All Phase 1-3 work
- Writing production code
- Writing tests (TDD)
- Component development
- Database migrations
- API implementation

BLOCKED:
- Production deployment

Input: Approved breakdown
Output: Working code with tests

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE 5: QA & Ship
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Purpose: Quality assurance and deployment

ALLOWED:
- Everything
- Code review
- Test review
- Performance testing
- Security audit
- Documentation
- Deployment

Input: Completed development
Output: Production-ready application

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE TRANSITIONS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To advance between phases:
1. Complete all phase requirements
2. Run: /gate-check (verify readiness)
3. Run: /phase-approve (human sign-off)
4. Run: /phase-advance (move to next phase)

Override: User can say "override: [reason]" - logged to .aid/overrides.log

================================================================================
3. STATE MANAGEMENT SYSTEM
================================================================================

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Phase State: .aid/state.json
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Tracks which phase the project is currently in.

Structure:
```json
{
  "current_phase": 1,
  "phase_name": "PRD",
  "started_at": "2025-12-11T10:00:00Z",
  "phases": {
    "1": { "status": "in_progress", "started_at": "...", "completed_at": null },
    "2": { "status": "locked" },
    "3": { "status": "locked" },
    "4": { "status": "locked" },
    "5": { "status": "locked" }
  },
  "artifacts": {
    "prd": "docs/PRD.md",
    "tech_spec": null,
    "breakdown": null
  }
}
```

Status Values:
- not_started: Phase hasn't begun
- in_progress: Currently active phase
- blocked: Waiting for dependency
- complete: Phase finished and approved
- locked: Cannot access until previous phases complete

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Work Context: .aid/context.json
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Real-time tracking of current work at task and step levels.

Structure:
```json
{
  "last_updated": "2025-12-11T14:30:00Z",
  "session_id": "2025-12-11-afternoon",

  "tasks": {
    "previous": {
      "key": "PROJ-123",
      "title": "Implement login form",
      "status": "done"
    },
    "current": {
      "key": "PROJ-124",
      "title": "Add password validation",
      "status": "in_progress"
    },
    "next": {
      "key": "PROJ-125",
      "title": "Create forgot password flow",
      "status": "todo"
    }
  },

  "current_task_steps": {
    "previous": {
      "name": "Write validation tests",
      "status": "done",
      "completed_at": "2025-12-11T13:45:00Z"
    },
    "current": {
      "name": "Implement validation logic",
      "status": "in_progress",
      "progress": "60%",
      "notes": "Regex patterns complete, strength meter pending"
    },
    "next": {
      "name": "Add error messages",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  },

  "blockers": [],

  "session_log": [
    {
      "timestamp": "2025-12-11T09:00:00Z",
      "action": "session_started",
      "details": "Morning startup via /good-morning"
    },
    {
      "timestamp": "2025-12-11T09:05:00Z",
      "action": "task_started",
      "details": "Started PROJ-124: Add password validation"
    }
  ]
}
```

Context Update Triggers:
- Before starting work (read context)
- Starting new task (shift tasks, reset steps)
- Starting new step (shift steps, add to log)
- Completing step (mark done, add notes)
- Completing task (mark done, shift all)
- End of session (save full state)

Session Log Actions:
- session_started
- session_ended
- task_started
- task_completed
- step_started
- step_completed
- progress_update
- blocker_noted
- context_saved

================================================================================
4. COMPLETE COMMAND REFERENCE
================================================================================

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SETUP COMMANDS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/setup
  Purpose: Complete guided setup for non-technical users
  When: First time using AID
  Action: Interactive installation wizard

/link-project [path]
  Purpose: Link existing project to AID via symbolic links
  When: Using AID with separate project folder
  Action: Creates symlinks for .claude/, CLAUDE.md, skills/

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DAILY WORKFLOW COMMANDS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/good-morning
  Purpose: Daily startup routine
  When: Beginning of each work session
  Action:
    1. Check Docker status
    2. Check MCP connections
    3. Load phase state
    4. Load work context
    5. Display where you left off
    6. Ask what to continue

/context
  Purpose: Show current work context
  When: Need to see current task/step
  Action: Display tasks.current and current_task_steps.current

/context-update
  Purpose: Manually update work context
  When: Progress made outside Claude Code
  Action: Interactive context modification

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PHASE MANAGEMENT COMMANDS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/aid-init
  Purpose: Initialize project with AID phases
  When: Starting new project
  Action: Create .aid/ directory with state.json and context.json

/phase
  Purpose: Show current phase status
  When: Check which phase you're in
  Action: Display current_phase, phase_name, progress

/gate-check
  Purpose: Verify readiness to advance
  When: Before requesting phase approval
  Action: Check all phase requirements are met

/phase-approve
  Purpose: Human sign-off for current phase
  When: Phase work is complete
  Action: Create approval file, update state

/phase-advance
  Purpose: Move to next phase
  When: After approval received
  Action: Update state.json, unlock next phase

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TESTING COMMANDS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/aid-test
  Purpose: Run full methodology test suite
  When: Verify AID installation works
  Action: Simulate full workflow with mock approvals

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DEVELOPMENT COMMANDS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

/prd [feature]
  Purpose: Create Product Requirements Document
  Phase: 1
  Skill: None
  Action: Interactive PRD creation from requirements

/tech-spec [feature]
  Purpose: Create Technical Specification
  Phase: 2
  Skill: system-architect
  Action: Architecture design from approved PRD

/jira-breakdown
  Purpose: Break spec into Jira issues
  Phase: 3
  Skill: None
  Action: Create epics, stories, tasks in Jira

/architecture
  Purpose: System architecture design
  Phase: 2
  Skill: system-architect
  Action: Design system architecture

/design-system
  Purpose: Build design system from Figma
  Phase: 4
  Skill: atomic-design
  Action: Extract tokens, create components

/build-page
  Purpose: Compose pages from components
  Phase: 4
  Skill: atomic-page-builder
  Action: Build pages using existing design system

/write-tests
  Purpose: Write tests using TDD
  Phase: 4
  Skill: test-driven
  Action: Create tests before implementation

/test-review [path]
  Purpose: Review test quality
  Phase: 5
  Skill: test-driven
  Action: Audit tests against TDD standards

/code-review [path]
  Purpose: Review code changes
  Phase: 5
  Skill: code-review
  Action: Comprehensive code quality review

/start-project [name]
  Purpose: Initialize new project
  Phase: Any
  Action: Create project structure

================================================================================
5. SKILLS SYSTEM (DETAILED)
================================================================================

Skills are specialized knowledge bases with workflows and prompts.
Location: skills/*/SKILL.md

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 1: Phase Enforcement (Always Active)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/phase-enforcement/SKILL.md

Purpose: Enforce phase gates - block work from future phases

Workflow:
1. Read .aid/state.json
2. Classify requested work by category
3. Check if category allowed in current phase
4. REFUSE if not allowed

Work Categories:
- requirements: PRD, scope, user stories
- architecture: Tech spec, schemas, APIs
- planning: Jira, task breakdown
- coding: Implementation, tests
- qa: Review, deployment

Phase Permissions Matrix:
| Phase | requirements | architecture | planning | coding | qa |
|-------|--------------|--------------|----------|--------|-----|
| 1     | вњ“            | вњ—            | вњ—        | вњ—      | вњ—   |
| 2     | вњ“            | вњ“            | вњ—        | вњ—      | вњ—   |
| 3     | вњ“            | вњ“            | вњ“        | вњ—      | вњ—   |
| 4     | вњ“            | вњ“            | вњ“        | вњ“      | вњ—   |
| 5     | вњ“            | вњ“            | вњ“        | вњ“      | вњ“   |

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 2: Context Tracking (Always Active)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/context-tracking/SKILL.md

Purpose: Maintain real-time work context for session continuity

Standard Component Development Steps:
1. Load task context
2. Read Tech Spec section
3. Write tests (TDD)
4. Implement component
5. Run tests / fix
6. Apply styling
7. Self code review
8. Update Jira
9. Commit

Update Protocol:
- BEFORE starting: Read context
- Starting task: Shift tasks, reset steps
- Starting step: Shift steps, log
- Completing step: Mark done, add notes
- Completing task: Mark done, shift all

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 3: System Architect (Phase 2)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/system-architect/SKILL.md

Purpose: Architecture design for distributed systems

Philosophy: "Make it work, make it right, make it fast - in that order"

Guiding Principles:
1. User journeys drive architecture
2. Simplicity first, scale when needed
3. Boring technology wins
4. Design for failure

Technology Defaults:
- Database: PostgreSQL
- Cache: Redis
- Queue: Redis/BullMQ or SQS
- API: REST + OpenAPI
- Auth: OAuth2 + JWT
- Cloud: AWS or GCP
- Container: Docker + ECS/Cloud Run
- Monitoring: Datadog or CloudWatch

Workflows:
1. Tech Spec Creation
   - Analyze PRD
   - Design data model
   - Define API contracts
   - Plan authentication
   - Document architecture

2. Architecture Design
   - Identify components
   - Define boundaries
   - Plan communication
   - Design for scale

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 4: Atomic Design (Phase 4)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/atomic-design/SKILL.md

Purpose: Build production design systems from Figma

Methodology: Brad Frost's Atomic Design

Hierarchy:
1. Design Tokens (primitives в†’ semantic)
2. Atoms (Button, Input, Typography, Icon)
3. Molecules (FormField, SearchBar, Card)
4. Organisms (Header, Form, DataTable)
5. Templates (DashboardLayout, AuthLayout)
6. Pages (Complete views)

Component Structure:
```
design-system/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tokens/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ primitives.ts    # Raw values
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ semantic.ts      # Contextual tokens
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ atoms/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Button/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Input/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Typography/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ molecules/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ FormField/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Card/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ organisms/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Header/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ Form/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ DashboardLayout/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ index.ts             # Public API
```

Key Rules:
- All visual styles encapsulated (NO visual props)
- Only content/behavior as props
- Full TypeScript types
- Accessibility built-in (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- Zero hardcoded colors/spacing/typography
- Responsive by default

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 5: Atomic Page Builder (Phase 4)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/atomic-page-builder/SKILL.md

Purpose: Compose pages using ONLY existing components

Dependency: Requires atomic-design skill

Core Principle: COMPOSE, NEVER CREATE

Workflow:
1. Component Inventory
   - List all existing components
   - Categorize by level

2. Gap Analysis
   - Identify needed components
   - Check if they exist

3. STOP if gaps exist
   - Switch to atomic-design skill
   - Create missing components first

4. Page Composition (if no gaps)
   - Import components
   - Compose layout
   - Pass content props only

Forbidden Patterns:
- Creating new atoms/molecules in page files
- Inline styles
- Hardcoded values (colors, spacing, fonts)
- External UI libraries
- CSS classes for styling

Output Checklist:
- [ ] All components from design system
- [ ] Zero hardcoded colors
- [ ] Zero hardcoded spacing
- [ ] Zero hardcoded typography
- [ ] Zero new component definitions
- [ ] Layout uses only design tokens

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 6: Code Review (Phase 4-5)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/code-review/SKILL.md

Purpose: Thorough review against professional standards

Review Checklists:

1. Pre-implementation Workflow
   - Was the task understood before coding?
   - Was design considered?

2. No Shortcuts
   - No silent try-except
   - No magic numbers
   - No copy-paste code

3. No Hardcoded Values
   - Colors from tokens
   - Strings externalized
   - Paths configurable

4. No Default Value Abuse
   - Defaults make sense
   - No hidden behavior

5. Root Cause Fixes
   - Fix cause, not symptom
   - No workarounds

6. Code Quality
   - Single responsibility
   - Clear naming
   - Appropriate abstraction

7. Architecture & Patterns
   - Consistent patterns
   - Proper boundaries

8. Testing & Validation
   - Tests exist
   - Edge cases covered

9. Documentation
   - Complex logic documented
   - API documented

Red Flags:
- Fragile code
- Quick fixes
- Performance shortcuts
- Security gaps
- Test manipulation

Verdict Options:
- Ready вњ“ - Ship it
- Needs Review вљ пёЏ - Minor issues
- Needs Rework вњ— - Major problems

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SKILL 7: Test-Driven Development (Phase 4-5)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
File: skills/test-driven/SKILL.md

Purpose: Write tests BEFORE implementation

TDD Workflow:
1. Understand requirements
2. Design test cases
3. Write tests FIRST (must fail - red)
4. Implement to pass (green)
5. Refactor (clean)

Minimal Mocking Strategy:
Target: <20% mocking in test suite

USE REAL:
- Database (test instance)
- Redis
- File system
- HTTP (test server)
- Time-based logic

MOCK ONLY:
- External paid APIs
- Non-deterministic services
- Third-party services with rate limits

Test Data Requirements:
- Realistic, production-like
- Not simplified "test" strings
- Proper dates, names, amounts
- Edge case values

Strong Assertions:
BAD:
```javascript
expect(result).toBeDefined()
expect(user).not.toBeNull()
```

GOOD:
```javascript
expect(result.status).toBe('success')
expect(user.email).toBe('john@example.com')
```

Coverage Checklist:
- [ ] Happy path
- [ ] Edge cases
- [ ] Error conditions
- [ ] Exception handling
- [ ] Async flows
- [ ] Integration scenarios
- [ ] Regression tests

Anti-Patterns to Avoid:
- Mock Hell: Excessive mocking
- Weak Assertions: toBeDefined, not toBeNull
- Simplified Data: "test", "foo", "bar"
- Test Modification: Changing tests to pass
- Overfitting: Tests tied to implementation
- Shared State: Tests affecting each other
- Hidden Failures: Silent catches in tests

================================================================================
6. MCP INTEGRATIONS (DETAILED)
================================================================================

Configuration: .mcp.json

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATLASSIAN (Jira/Confluence)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Type: SSE (Server-Sent Events)
URL: https://mcp.atlassian.com/v1/sse

Purpose:
- Create/manage Jira epics
- Create stories and tasks
- Update issue status
- Access Confluence docs

Setup:
1. Go to .mcp.json
2. SSE connection is pre-configured
3. OAuth authentication in browser when first used

Capabilities:
- jira_create_issue
- jira_update_issue
- jira_search
- jira_get_issue
- confluence_get_page
- confluence_create_page

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GITHUB
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Command: npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-github
Env Var: GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN

Purpose:
- Repository operations
- Pull request management
- Issue tracking
- Code review

Setup:
1. Create Personal Access Token at github.com/settings/tokens
2. Add to .env: GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_xxx

Capabilities:
- github_create_repo
- github_create_pr
- github_merge_pr
- github_create_issue
- github_get_file
- github_push_files

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FIGMA
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Command: npx figma-developer-mcp
Env Var: FIGMA_API_KEY

Purpose:
- Extract design tokens
- Get component specs
- Export assets
- Read styles

Setup Options:

Option A: API-Based
1. Go to Figma в†’ Settings в†’ Account в†’ Personal access tokens
2. Create token
3. Add to .env: FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_xxx

Option B: Dev Mode Local
1. Open Figma file
2. Press Shift+D for Dev Mode
3. Enable MCP server in Dev Mode settings
4. Local connection established

Capabilities:
- figma_get_file
- figma_get_styles
- figma_get_components
- figma_export_images

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
CHROME DEVTOOLS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Command: npx chrome-devtools-mcp@latest

Purpose:
- Browser automation
- UI testing
- Performance audits
- Screenshot capture

Use Cases:
1. Development
   - Live UI verification
   - Console monitoring
   - Network inspection

2. Testing
   - Screenshot comparison
   - E2E flow automation
   - Visual regression

3. Review
   - Performance audits
   - Core Web Vitals
   - Accessibility checks

Test Artifacts: Saved to test-recordings/

Capabilities:
- chrome_navigate
- chrome_screenshot
- chrome_click
- chrome_type
- chrome_evaluate
- chrome_get_console
- chrome_performance_audit

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
POSTGRESQL
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Command: npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres
Env Var: DATABASE_URL

Purpose:
- Database queries
- Schema inspection
- Data management

Setup:
1. Add to .env: DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:pass@host:5432/db

Capabilities:
- postgres_query
- postgres_get_schema
- postgres_list_tables

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FILESYSTEM
------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Command: npx @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem
Root: Current directory

Purpose:
- File operations
- Directory management

Capabilities:
- fs_read_file
- fs_write_file
- fs_list_directory
- fs_create_directory

================================================================================
7. PROJECT STRUCTURE & FILES
================================================================================

```
AID/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .claude/                          # Claude Code integration
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ commands/                     # Slash commands (21 files)
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ aid-init.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ aid-test.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ architecture.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ build-page.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ code-review.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ context-update.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ context.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ design-system.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ gate-check.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ good-morning.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ jira-breakdown.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ link-project.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ phase-advance.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ phase-approve.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ phase.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ prd.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ setup.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ start-project.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tech-spec.md
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ test-review.md
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ write-tests.md
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ settings.local.json           # Local Claude settings
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .mcp.json                         # MCP server configuration
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ skills/                           # Specialized skills
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ phase-enforcement/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ context-tracking/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ system-architect/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ references/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ atomic-design/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ references/
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ atomic-methodology.md
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ component-structure.md
в”‚   в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ design-tokens.md
в”‚   в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ figma-mcp-integration.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ atomic-page-builder/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ code-review/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ references/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ test-driven/
в”‚       в”њв”Ђв”Ђ SKILL.md
в”‚       в””в”Ђв”Ђ references/
в”‚           в”њв”Ђв”Ђ anti-patterns.md
в”‚           в””в”Ђв”Ђ review-checklist.md
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docs/                             # Documentation
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ PHASE-GATES.md                # Phase system details
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ WORK-CONTEXT-TRACKER.md       # Context tracking system
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ MORNING-STARTUP.md            # Daily workflow
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ TEST-SCENARIOS.md             # Testing workflows
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ UPGRADE-GUIDE.md              # Version upgrades
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mcp-setup.md                  # MCP configuration
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ phases/
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ overview.md               # 8-phase detailed guide
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ prompts/                      # Prompt templates
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ integrations/                     # MCP integration guides
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ jira/README.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ github/README.md
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ figma/README.md
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ chrome-devtools/README.md
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ templates/                        # Project templates
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .aid/
в”‚   в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ state.json.template
в”‚   в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ context.json.template
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ prd-template.md
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ scripts/                          # Automation
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ init-project.sh
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ startup-check.sh
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ migrate.js
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ create-migration.js
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ generate-keys.js
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ src/                              # Example application
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app/                          # Next.js app
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ components/                   # React components
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ styles/                       # SCSS styles
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ prisma/                           # Database ORM
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ schema.prisma
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ public/                           # Static assets
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ testing/                          # Test utilities
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ test-recordings/                  # Browser test artifacts
в”‚
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ CLAUDE.md                         # Critical Claude instructions
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ README.md                         # Main documentation
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ DEPENDENCIES.md                   # Dependency guide
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ package.json                      # Node dependencies
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ .env.example                      # Environment template
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ tsconfig.json                     # TypeScript config
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ jest.config.js                    # Jest config
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ next.config.js                    # Next.js config
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Makefile                          # Build automation
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ Dockerfile                        # Container definition
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ docker-compose.yml                # Multi-container setup
в””в”Ђв”Ђ install.sh                        # Installation script
```

================================================================================
8. TECHNOLOGY STACK
================================================================================

FRONTEND
- Framework: Next.js 16
- Library: React 19
- Language: TypeScript 5.9
- Styling: SCSS (CSS Modules)
- UI System: Atomic Design

BACKEND
- Runtime: Node.js 18+
- Framework: Next.js API Routes
- Language: TypeScript
- Authentication: JWT + bcryptjs

DATABASE
- Primary: PostgreSQL 16+
- ORM: Prisma 6.1
- Client: pg
- Dev Environment: Docker Compose

TESTING
- Framework: Jest 29
- React: @testing-library/react
- DOM: @testing-library/jest-dom
- E2E: Puppeteer 24
- API: Supertest
- TypeScript: ts-jest

BUILD & DEPLOYMENT
- Bundler: Next.js (built-in)
- Container: Docker
- Orchestration: Docker Compose
- Package Manager: npm

UTILITIES
- Class Names: clsx
- SVG: @svgr/webpack
- Environment: dotenv

================================================================================
9. INSTALLATION & SETUP
================================================================================

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
NON-TECHNICAL USERS (Recommended)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. Download Claude Code Desktop
2. Download/extract AID folder
3. Open folder in Claude Code
4. Type: /setup
5. Follow interactive guide

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TECHNICAL USERS
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Clone repository
git clone https://github.com/ilandahan/AID.git
cd AID

# Run installation
./install.sh

# Or use Makefile
make install

# IMPORTANT: Restart Claude Code after installation

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MANUAL SETUP
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Install dependencies
npm install

# Pre-install MCP servers
npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-github
npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres
npm install -g @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem
npm install -g figma-developer-mcp
npm install -g chrome-devtools-mcp

# Copy environment file
cp .env.example .env

# Edit .env with your credentials
# - GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN
# - FIGMA_API_KEY
# - DATABASE_URL
# - ATLASSIAN credentials (if using)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PROJECT LINKING (Separate Projects)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Structure:
```
workspace/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ AID/                    в†ђ This repo (shared tools)
в””в”Ђв”Ђ my-project/             в†ђ Your project
```

Mac/Linux:
```bash
cd my-project
ln -s ../AID/.claude .claude
ln -s ../AID/CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md
ln -s ../AID/skills skills
mkdir -p .aid
```

Windows (PowerShell as Admin):
```powershell
cd my-project
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path ".claude" -Target "..\AID\.claude"
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "CLAUDE.md" -Target "..\AID\CLAUDE.md"
New-Item -ItemType SymbolicLink -Path "skills" -Target "..\AID\skills"
mkdir .aid
```

Or use: /link-project [path]

================================================================================
10. DAILY WORKFLOW
================================================================================

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MORNING STARTUP (/good-morning)
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. System Checks
   - Docker status
   - MCP connections (Figma, Jira, GitHub)
   - Database connection

2. Context Loading
   - Read .aid/state.json (phase)
   - Read .aid/context.json (tasks)

3. Status Display
   - Current phase
   - Current task
   - Current step
   - Progress notes

4. Options Presented
   a. Continue current step
   b. Mark step complete, move to next
   c. Mark task complete, move to next task
   d. Switch to different task
   e. Offline update (describe what you did)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------
TYPICAL DEVELOPMENT DAY
------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Morning:
```
/good-morning
> Continue current step
```

During Work:
```
# Check context anytime
/context

# Update progress
/context-update
```

Completing Steps:
```
# Step done
/context-update step done

# Task done
/context-update task done
```

End of Day:
```
# Context auto-saves
# Or explicitly save notes
/context-update notes "Stopped mid-refactor, tests passing"
```

================================================================================
11. DEVELOPMENT STANDARDS
================================================================================

CODE QUALITY REQUIREMENTS

1. No Silent Failures
   BAD:  try { ... } catch { }
   GOOD: try { ... } catch(e) { logger.error(e); throw e; }

2. No Magic Numbers
   BAD:  if (status === 3)
   GOOD: if (status === Status.APPROVED)

3. No Hardcoded Values
   BAD:  color: '#FF5733'
   GOOD: color: tokens.colors.primary

4. No Copy-Paste Code
   BAD:  Duplicate logic in multiple places
   GOOD: Extract to shared function/component

5. Single Responsibility
   BAD:  Function does 5 things
   GOOD: Function does 1 thing well

6. Clear Naming
   BAD:  const d = getData()
   GOOD: const userData = fetchUserProfile()

7. Proper Error Handling
   BAD:  Swallow errors
   GOOD: Handle, log, and propagate appropriately

8. No TODOs in Production
   BAD:  // TODO: fix this later
   GOOD: Complete the implementation

================================================================================
12. TESTING STANDARDS
================================================================================

TDD WORKFLOW

1. RED: Write failing test
2. GREEN: Write minimal code to pass
3. REFACTOR: Clean up while tests pass

MOCKING GUIDELINES

Mock (<20% of tests):
- External paid APIs (Stripe, SendGrid)
- Non-deterministic services
- Rate-limited third-party services

Use Real:
- Database (test instance)
- Redis (test instance)
- File system (temp directory)
- HTTP (test server)
- Time (control with libraries)

TEST DATA

BAD:
```javascript
const user = { name: 'test', email: 'test@test.com' }
```

GOOD:
```javascript
const user = {
  name: 'Sarah Johnson',
  email: 'sarah.johnson@company.com',
  createdAt: new Date('2024-03-15T10:30:00Z'),
  role: 'admin'
}
```

ASSERTIONS

BAD:
```javascript
expect(result).toBeDefined()
expect(user).toBeTruthy()
expect(items.length).toBeGreaterThan(0)
```

GOOD:
```javascript
expect(result.status).toBe('success')
expect(user.email).toBe('sarah@company.com')
expect(items).toHaveLength(3)
expect(items[0].name).toBe('First Item')
```

COVERAGE REQUIREMENTS

- Happy path: 100%
- Error conditions: 100%
- Edge cases: As many as reasonable
- Integration: Key flows

================================================================================
13. ATOMIC DESIGN SYSTEM
================================================================================

HIERARCHY

1. DESIGN TOKENS
   - Primitives: Raw values (colors, spacing numbers)
   - Semantic: Contextual meaning (primary, danger, spacing-md)

2. ATOMS
   - Smallest UI elements
   - Button, Input, Typography, Icon, Badge

3. MOLECULES
   - Combinations of atoms
   - FormField (Label + Input + Error)
   - SearchBar (Input + Button)
   - Card (Container + Content)

4. ORGANISMS
   - Complex UI sections
   - Header (Logo + Nav + UserMenu)
   - Form (Multiple FormFields + Submit)
   - DataTable (Headers + Rows + Pagination)

5. TEMPLATES
   - Page layouts
   - DashboardLayout (Sidebar + Header + Content)
   - AuthLayout (Centered card)

6. PAGES
   - Complete views
   - Specific content in templates

COMPONENT RULES

DO:
- Use only design tokens for visual styles
- Accept only content/behavior props
- Include TypeScript types
- Build in accessibility
- Support all breakpoints

DON'T:
- Accept color/size/spacing props
- Hardcode any values
- Import external UI libraries
- Create inline styles
- Skip TypeScript

EXAMPLE COMPONENT

```typescript
// atoms/Button/Button.tsx
import styles from './Button.module.scss'

interface ButtonProps {
  children: React.ReactNode
  onClick?: () => void
  type?: 'button' | 'submit'
  disabled?: boolean
}

export function Button({
  children,
  onClick,
  type = 'button',
  disabled = false
}: ButtonProps) {
  return (
    <button
      className={styles.button}
      onClick={onClick}
      type={type}
      disabled={disabled}
    >
      {children}
    </button>
  )
}
```

```scss
// atoms/Button/Button.module.scss
@use '../../tokens' as *;

.button {
  background: $color-primary;
  color: $color-on-primary;
  padding: $spacing-sm $spacing-md;
  border-radius: $radius-md;
  font-family: $font-family-base;
  font-size: $font-size-md;

  &:hover {
    background: $color-primary-hover;
  }

  &:disabled {
    background: $color-disabled;
    cursor: not-allowed;
  }
}
```

================================================================================
14. CODE QUALITY RULES
================================================================================

PROHIBITED PATTERNS

1. Silent Exception Handling
   ```javascript
   // PROHIBITED
   try { doSomething() } catch {}

   // REQUIRED
   try { doSomething() } catch(e) {
     logger.error('Operation failed', e)
     throw new OperationError('Failed to do something', e)
   }
   ```

2. Commented-Out Code
   ```javascript
   // PROHIBITED
   // const oldImplementation = () => {...}

   // REQUIRED: Delete unused code, use git history
   ```

3. TODO/FIXME Comments
   ```javascript
   // PROHIBITED in production
   // TODO: handle edge case

   // REQUIRED: Complete implementation before merge
   ```

4. Hardcoded Strings
   ```javascript
   // PROHIBITED
   return 'User not found'

   // REQUIRED
   return messages.USER_NOT_FOUND
   ```

5. Magic Numbers
   ```javascript
   // PROHIBITED
   if (retries > 3)

   // REQUIRED
   if (retries > MAX_RETRIES)
   ```

6. Default Parameter Abuse
   ```javascript
   // PROHIBITED
   function fetch(url, timeout = 0) // 0 means infinite??

   // REQUIRED
   function fetch(url, timeout = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS)
   ```

================================================================================
15. ENVIRONMENT CONFIGURATION
================================================================================

Copy .env.example to .env and configure:

```bash
# =============================================================================
# ATLASSIAN (Jira / Confluence)
# =============================================================================
ATLASSIAN_SITE_URL=https://your-domain.atlassian.net
ATLASSIAN_USER_EMAIL=your-email@company.com
ATLASSIAN_API_TOKEN=your-api-token

# =============================================================================
# GITHUB
# =============================================================================
GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=ghp_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# =============================================================================
# FIGMA
# =============================================================================
FIGMA_API_KEY=figd_xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx

# =============================================================================
# DATABASE
# =============================================================================
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/database

# =============================================================================
# APPLICATION
# =============================================================================
JWT_SECRET=your-secret-key
NODE_ENV=development
```

================================================================================
16. TROUBLESHOOTING
================================================================================

COMMON ISSUES

1. "Phase violation" error
   - Check current phase: /phase
   - Requested work is for a future phase
   - Complete current phase first, or use override

2. MCP connection failed
   - Check .mcp.json configuration
   - Verify environment variables in .env
   - Restart Claude Code

3. Context not loading
   - Check .aid/context.json exists
   - Verify JSON syntax
   - Run /aid-init to recreate

4. Commands not recognized
   - Check .claude/commands/ exists
   - Verify symbolic links if using linked project
   - Restart Claude Code

5. Tests failing
   - Don't modify tests to pass
   - Fix implementation instead
   - Check test database connection

6. Docker not running
   - Start Docker Desktop
   - Run: docker-compose up -d
   - Check logs: docker-compose logs

GETTING HELP

- Documentation: docs/*.md
- GitHub Issues: https://github.com/ilandahan/AID/issues
- Slash command help: /help

================================================================================
END OF DOCUMENTATION
================================================================================
